fixture.description: New code sorts candidates by descending score then inserts each with add(0, ...), reversing the order so the lowest-priority item ends up first. Double.compare itself is correct — the bug is the interaction.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that sorting candidates by descending score and then inserting every element with `target.add(0, c)` (head insertion) reverses the intended order, so the lowest-score candidate ends up at index 0 (tried first) — inverted priority; the fix is to append at the tail or flip the sort direction. Acceptable severity: IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the order-inversion is not surfaced. The comparator `Double.compare(b, a)` is itself correct (no overflow) and should NOT be flagged as a broken comparator.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags the sort-direction vs head-insertion order inversion
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim Double.compare itself is a broken comparator
